free_text: 236Pu(n,f), 237Pu(n,f), and 238Pu(n,f) cross sections
deduced from (p,t), (p,d), and (p,p') surrogate
reactions
University of Richmond, Richmond, Virginia
Cross sections
ratios of surrogate reactions induced by charged
particles, from which cross sections in subs 2,3,4
are determined, are compiled in entry C2153
Experiment performed at the K-150
cyclotron facility at Texas A&M University
Measurement performed using STARLiTeR setup,
consisting of Silicon Telescope Array for Reaction
Studies (STARS) and the Livermore-Texas-Richmond array
LiTeR of up to six Compton-suppressed clover gamma-
ray detectors
STARS consisted of one 140 mu-m dE and
two 1000 mu-m Micron S2 type silicon detectors placed
downstream of the target for the detection of
outgoing protons, deuterons and tritons between 36deg
and 67deg. Another 140 mu-m Micron S2 type detector
was placed upstream for the detection of coincident
fission fragments at backward angles between 108deg
and 135deg. Each S2 detector had 8 sectors and 24
1-mm-wide rings to provide position sensitivity in
measuring incident charged particles
LiTeR array consists of up to six Compton-
suppressed clover gamma-ray detectors, of which five
were used in the present experiment.
Particle were identified using E-dE method. In
the energy range relevant to the (p,df) and (p,tf)
reactions, all particles were stopped in the E1
detector, the E2 detector was needed for stopping
protons and higher energy deuterons.
In surrogate method enriched targets 235U and 239Pu
were used. Target thickness ratio 235U/239Pu was
measured using alpha particle spectra and was found
2.58+-0.18. For both targets, contaminant isotopic
species are less than 1%.
Compiled by S.H.
Total uncertainty, including
- statistical uncertainties
- systematic uncertainties
- uncertainties in CN formation cross calculations
Data at the approximate En values, where the outgoing
light ion energy falls below nominal Coulomb barrier
are considered as unreliable, based on previous
surrogate measurement, which deviate from the directly
measured (n,f) cross section at equivalent neutron
energies. Unreliable data are marked with flag.
Data taken from fig.11b of the reference
waiting for data from author
Surrogate ratio method was used. Two surrogate
measurements in identical setup with two different
targets 235U and 239Pu were performed. Surrogate
reactions were (p,t). From ratio of cross sections
235U(p,tf)/239Pu(p,tf) cross section of 236Pu(n,f)
was determined
Data considered as unreliable
Data taken from fig. 9b of the reference
waiting for data from author
Surrogate ratio method was used. Two surrogate
measurements in identical setup with two different
targets 235U and 239Pu were performed. Surrogate
reactions were (p,d). From ratio of cross sections
235U(p,df)/239Pu(p,df) cross section of 237Pu(n,f)
was determined
Data considered as unreliable
Data taken from fig.12 of the reference
waiting for data from author
Surrogate ratio method was used. Two surrogate
measurements in identical setup with two different
targets 235U and 239Pu were performed. Surrogate
reactions were (p,p'). From ratio of cross sections
235U(p,p'f)/239Pu(p,p'f) cross section of 238Pu(n,f)
was determined.
Cross section determined from surrogate ratio
relative to ENDF/B-VII evaluation of 234U(n,f)
cross section.
Data considered as unreliable
